appointment-booking-scheduling.headline: Customer books. CRM updates. Deal stages sync themselves.
appointment-booking-scheduling.dek: When someone schedules through your appointment scheduler, HubSpot automatically updates contact records, deal stages, and project types. No phone tag, no double-booking, no manual entry.

beats.label: Customer books appointment
beats.detail: Appointment scheduler sends booking event with customer email, project type, and time slot.

beats.label: Find the contact owner
beats.detail: System checks who owns this customer relationship and pulls their project type preferences.

beats.label: Build the deal payload
beats.detail: Combines booking details with contact data and project specifications into a structured record.

beats.label: Does this deal exist?
beats.detail: Checks if there's already a HubSpot deal for this customer and project type.

beats.label: Create new deal
beats.detail: Sets deal stage to 'Appointment Scheduled' and links the contact record with project details.

beats.label: Update existing deal
beats.detail: Moves deal to appropriate pipeline stage and updates appointment time without creating duplicates.

beats.label: Sync contact record
beats.detail: Updates HubSpot contact with latest booking information and project preferences.

beats.label: Deal stage updated
beats.detail: HubSpot shows current appointment status and the sales team sees exactly where each customer stands.

lead-capture-qualification.headline: Lead comes in. Data gets enriched. HubSpot gets updated.
lead-capture-qualification.dek: Every form submission flows through 24 steps of lookup, enrichment, and deduplication before landing in your CRM as a complete contact record with deal and source data attached.

beats.label: Lead data arrives
beats.detail: Form submission hits the system from any marketing channel. Could be 2pm or 2am — the automation doesn't sleep.

beats.label: Project details surface
beats.detail: System pulls project type and specifications from the original lead source. Residential remodel or commercial build — the context matters.

beats.label: Marketing source traced
beats.detail: Which campaign, which vendor, which channel drove this lead? The attribution data gets captured and preserved.

beats.label: Contact exists already?
beats.detail: HubSpot search runs by email and contact ID. Existing customer or brand new prospect — the system knows the difference.

beats.label: Update existing contact
beats.detail: Merge new project details with existing contact properties. No duplicate records, no lost history.

beats.label: Create fresh contact
beats.detail: New contact record gets built with full project context and marketing attribution from day one.

beats.label: Deal record attached
beats.detail: Contact gets paired with a deal record containing project type, estimated value, and original lead source data.

beats.label: HubSpot updated completely
beats.detail: Clean contact record with attached deal and full marketing attribution. Ready for sales follow-up within minutes of form submission.

estimate-to-deal-pipeline.headline: Your estimating tool talks. HubSpot listens. The pipeline updates itself.
estimate-to-deal-pipeline.dek: The moment you create an estimate, HubSpot gets a perfectly named deal with the right value. No copying numbers between systems, no manual pipeline updates.

beats.label: Estimate gets created
beats.detail: New quote appears in your estimating tool. Customer name, project scope, dollar amount — all sitting there waiting to become a deal.

beats.label: Check recent estimates
beats.detail: System pulls all quotes from the last few minutes. Catches everything, even if multiple estimates come in at once.

beats.label: Loop through each quote
beats.detail: Every estimate gets processed individually. Johnson Kitchen Remodel, $12,500. Martinez Bathroom, $8,200.

beats.label: Already in HubSpot?
beats.detail: Checks if this exact estimate already exists as a deal. Prevents duplicates when the system runs multiple times.

beats.label: Create HubSpot deal
beats.detail: New deal appears with consistent naming: customer name, project type, estimate value. Sales team sees it instantly in their pipeline.

beats.label: Pipeline stays current
beats.detail: Every estimate becomes a trackable deal automatically. Sales managers get accurate pipeline reports without touching a spreadsheet.

change-order-management.headline: Change order hits. Numbers aggregate. Deal value updates itself.
change-order-management.dek: When scope changes mid-project, this 44-step automation pulls all related HubSpot deals, sums amounts across labor/materials/discounts, and updates the master deal with real totals.

beats.label: Change order arrives
beats.detail: HubSpot deal gets updated with new scope. Could be extra materials, added labor hours, or discount adjustments.

beats.label: Find the deal family
beats.detail: Searches HubSpot for all deals tied to the same job record. Original estimate plus every change order so far.

beats.label: Sum across categories
beats.detail: Loops through each related deal, pulling amounts for labor, materials, discounts, and hours. Adds them all up.

beats.label: Project exists?
beats.detail: Checks if there's already a master project record in the estimating tool to sync the new totals to.

beats.label: Create missing project
beats.detail: No project found. Creates new record in estimating tool with aggregated totals from all change orders.

beats.label: Update existing project
beats.detail: Project already exists. Updates the estimating tool record with the new combined totals from HubSpot.

beats.label: Sync deal owners
beats.detail: Makes sure the right sales rep is assigned to both the HubSpot deal and estimating tool project.

beats.label: Real totals everywhere
beats.detail: HubSpot deal shows actual project value. Estimating tool reflects all changes. No more guessing what the job is really worth.

contract-lifecycle.headline: Contract signed. Back office spinning. Job starts itself.
contract-lifecycle.dek: The moment your customer signs digitally, the entire job setup process kicks off automatically. No email checking, no manual handoffs — just seamless contract-to-kickoff flow.

beats.label: Contract gets signed
beats.detail: Customer signs on their phone. Digital signature hits your system instantly.

beats.label: Pull contract records
beats.detail: System grabs the signed contract data and all related job details from your database.

beats.label: Check for addendums
beats.detail: Loops through any contract addendums or change orders. Formats dates and pulls each addendum record.

beats.label: What type of job?
beats.detail: Branches based on contract details — residential repaint, commercial new construction, or specialty work.

beats.label: Residential setup
beats.detail: Triggers crew scheduling, material orders, and homeowner communication sequence.

beats.label: Commercial setup
beats.detail: Initiates project management workflow, permit tracking, and contractor coordination.

beats.label: Job officially live
beats.detail: All setup processes complete. Job moves from signed contract to active project status.

beats.label: Back office running
beats.detail: Crew gets scheduled, materials get ordered, customer gets timeline. Zero manual handoff needed.

deal-to-job-processing.headline: Deal closes. Job opens. Addendums follow.
deal-to-job-processing.dek: The moment your CRM marks a deal won, the system parses job details, pulls every linked change order, and processes the full conversion in 10 automated steps.

beats.label: Deal closes in CRM
beats.detail: HubSpot sends the won deal event with customer data and job specifications.

beats.label: Parse the job data
beats.detail: System extracts customer info, project scope, and contract details from the deal record.

beats.label: Find all addendums
beats.detail: Searches for every change order and addendum linked to this deal ID.

beats.label: Check addendum status
beats.detail: Does this change order already have a job assigned to it?

beats.label: Skip assigned orders
beats.detail: Change order already has a job record. Move to the next one.

beats.label: Process new addendum
beats.detail: Creates job record for unassigned change orders with full project data.

beats.label: Job records created
beats.detail: All change orders now have proper job assignments in the system.

production-crew-tracking.headline: Crews work. Data flows. Office knows.
production-crew-tracking.dek: Time logs and project updates pull automatically from the field, match against active jobs, and sync to the office. No more calling crews for status reports.

beats.label: Production update arrives
beats.detail: Time tracking app sends hourly data. Crew hours, project codes, current status.

beats.label: Pull all time logs
beats.detail: Grab every entry from the last sync. Clock-in times, job sites, crew member names.

beats.label: Match active projects
beats.detail: Cross-reference time entries against live project database. Find the right job numbers.

beats.label: Project exists?
beats.detail: Does this time log match a real project in the system?

beats.label: Process valid data
beats.detail: Update project hours, calculate progress, sync to office dashboard.

beats.label: Skip orphaned logs
beats.detail: Time entry doesn't match any active project. Log the error and move on.

beats.label: Sync to office systems
beats.detail: Push updated production data to accounting software and project management tools.

time-tracking-sync-engine.headline: Someone clocks in. Job codes update. Payroll gets clean data.
time-tracking-sync-engine.dek: Every time entry, correction, and deletion flows between your time tracking app and project management system automatically. No more manual reconciliation or phantom hours in payroll.

beats.label: Employee clocks in
beats.detail: Time entry hits your time tracking app. Could be a regular shift start, overtime correction, or project switch mid-day.

beats.label: Pull fresh job codes
beats.detail: System grabs all active job codes and project data. Makes sure new sites and departments are available for assignment.

beats.label: Sync employee profiles
beats.detail: New hires get added automatically. Existing employees get updated roles, locations, and permissions across both systems.

beats.label: Entry deleted or updated?
beats.detail: System checks if this is a correction, deletion, or brand new time entry that needs processing.

beats.label: Clean up deleted entries
beats.detail: Removes phantom time logs from both systems. Prevents deleted entries from showing up in payroll reports.

beats.label: Update active time logs
beats.detail: Pushes new entries and corrections to project management. Job codes, locations, and hours all stay in sync.

beats.label: Reconcile all systems
beats.detail: Both paths converge to ensure time tracking app and project system show identical data.

beats.label: Payroll gets clean data
beats.detail: Hours, job codes, and employee assignments flow to payroll automatically. No manual reconciliation needed.

invoice-lifecycle.headline: Unbilled work gets caught. Invoices sync everywhere. Nothing slips through.
invoice-lifecycle.dek: Your accounting software and project management system stay perfectly in sync — unbilled line items become invoices automatically, modifications flow both ways, and voided invoices disappear from all dashboards.

beats.label: Unbilled work detected
beats.detail: System scans for line items that haven't been invoiced yet. Could be completed project phases or time entries sitting in your project management system.

beats.label: Pull customer details
beats.detail: Grabs the full customer record from your accounting software. Gets billing address, payment terms, and project type to build the invoice correctly.

beats.label: Build invoice automatically
beats.detail: Creates the invoice in your accounting software with all line items, custom fields, and proper formatting. No manual data entry required.

beats.label: Invoice created successfully?
beats.detail: Checks if the invoice was built without errors in your accounting software before proceeding to sync it elsewhere.

beats.label: Sync to project system
beats.detail: Pushes the new invoice into your project management system. Links it to the right job and updates billing status.

beats.label: Handle modifications
beats.detail: Catches changes made to existing invoices — edits, voids, or payment updates. Syncs the changes in both directions.

beats.label: Save invoice PDF
beats.detail: Generates and stores the PDF version of the invoice for your records and customer delivery.

beats.label: Everything stays synced
beats.detail: Both systems show the same invoice data. Voided invoices disappear from project dashboards. Payment status updates everywhere.

reporting-dashboard-sync.headline: Your CRM updates. Your dashboard refreshes. The numbers never lie.
reporting-dashboard-sync.dek: Every two minutes, deal data flows from HubSpot to your reporting dashboard. No manual pulls. No stale numbers. Just live pipeline health that updates itself.

beats.label: Clock strikes two
beats.detail: Every 120 seconds, the sync kicks off. No waiting for Monday morning reports.

beats.label: Generate deal payload
beats.detail: Pulls fresh deal records from HubSpot. Stage changes, close dates, amounts — everything that moved.

beats.label: New or changed?
beats.detail: Compares timestamps to find what actually needs updating. Skip the noise, sync what matters.

beats.label: Process deletions
beats.detail: Deals that got removed from HubSpot disappear from the dashboard too. Clean slate, accurate counts.

beats.label: Upsert records
beats.detail: New deals get added, existing ones get updated. One operation handles both cases.

beats.label: Dashboard refreshed
beats.detail: Live pipeline numbers replace last week's stale data. Managers see reality, not history.

communication-hub.headline: Someone books. Systems sync. Everyone knows.
communication-hub.dek: When a booking comes in, this automation finds the right deal in HubSpot, checks if the contact exists, and fires off the exact communication each system needs — no manual forwarding, no missed updates.

beats.label: Booking arrives
beats.detail: Someone schedules through your appointment scheduler. The event data hits the automation instantly.

beats.label: Find accepted quotes
beats.detail: Pulls all quotes marked as accepted from your estimating tool. Matches them against the booking details.

beats.label: Loop through quotes
beats.detail: Each quote gets processed individually. Converts estimate dates and preps the data for HubSpot sync.

beats.label: Get latest open deal
beats.detail: Searches HubSpot for the most recent open deal tied to this customer. Uses deal stage and contact info to match.

beats.label: Does contact exist?
beats.detail: Checks if this person already has a HubSpot contact record. Branches based on what it finds.

beats.label: Create new contact
beats.detail: No existing record found. Creates a fresh HubSpot contact with booking details and quote information.

beats.label: Update existing contact
beats.detail: Contact exists. Updates their record with new booking data and links it to the open deal.

beats.label: Systems stay synced
beats.detail: HubSpot, your estimating tool, and appointment scheduler all reflect the same information. No manual data entry required.

hubspot-contact-sync.headline: New lead arrives. HubSpot searches twice. One contact emerges.
hubspot-contact-sync.dek: Every marketing channel dumps leads into HubSpot differently. This 24-step flow searches by ID first, email second, then merges or creates — zero duplicates, zero manual hunting.

beats.label: Lead data arrives
beats.detail: Marketing vendor drops contact info into the system. Could be a webform, channel partner, or lead gen tool.

beats.label: Search by contact ID
beats.detail: HubSpot hunts for an existing contact using the unique ID. First line of defense against duplicates.

beats.label: Found or ghost?
beats.detail: If the ID search hits, we have a match. If not, time for plan B.

beats.label: Search by email instead
beats.detail: No ID match? HubSpot tries the email address. Different vendors use different identifiers.

beats.label: Merge existing contact
beats.detail: Contact found by ID. Pull current properties and merge with the fresh lead data.

beats.label: Final contact payload
beats.detail: Whether found by ID, email, or created fresh, all contact data gets standardized into one clean record.

beats.label: HubSpot contact updated
beats.detail: Contact exists in HubSpot with merged data from all sources. Sales reps see one clean record, not duplicates.

message-transpiler.headline: Customer message triggers. Deal data flows. Perfect link delivers.
message-transpiler.dek: Every outbound message automatically pulls the latest deal context, generates a personalized booking link, and shortens it. Customers get the right link in seconds, not minutes.

beats.label: Message goes out
beats.detail: Staff hits send on a customer message. The system catches this event and starts pulling context.

beats.label: Find the deal
beats.detail: System searches for the customer's latest open deal in HubSpot. Grabs current stage, value, and project details.

beats.label: Generate booking link
beats.detail: Creates a personalized appointment scheduler link based on the deal context. Different deal stages get different booking flows.

beats.label: Shorten the URL
beats.detail: Converts the long booking link into a clean, trackable short URL. Makes messages cleaner and tracks clicks.

beats.label: Which message format?
beats.detail: Checks deal stage and customer type to determine the right message template and tone.

beats.label: New prospect path
beats.detail: Formats message for first-time customers with introduction copy and basic booking instructions.

beats.label: Existing customer path
beats.detail: Uses familiar tone with deal-specific details and streamlined booking flow for repeat customers.

beats.label: Perfect message ready
beats.detail: Final message includes deal context, personalized booking link, and proper formatting. Ready to send in seconds.

expense-management-pipeline.headline: Bills arrive. Jobs get matched. Costs track themselves.
expense-management-pipeline.dek: Every expense from your accounting software automatically finds its project in Airtable by cross-referencing customer records and job data — with receipt attachments downloaded and linked.

beats.label: New Bill Arrives
beats.detail: Expense data flows in from your accounting software. Could be materials for the Henderson kitchen or fuel for the crew truck.

beats.label: Find the Customer
beats.detail: System searches for matching customer references across your job records. Links the vendor bill to the right client account.

beats.label: Download Receipt Files
beats.detail: Grabs any attached receipts or invoices from the expense record. Photos, PDFs, whatever the vendor sent.

beats.label: Job or Project?
beats.detail: Checks whether this expense belongs to a main job or a specific project phase within that job.

beats.label: Match to Job
beats.detail: Links the expense directly to the main job record in Airtable with full customer context.

beats.label: Match to Project
beats.detail: Connects the expense to the specific project phase, maintaining the parent job relationship.

beats.label: Update Records
beats.detail: Saves the expense data and attachment links to Airtable. Customer, job, and project connections all locked in.

beats.label: Cost Tracking Complete
beats.detail: Job costing is current without the treasure hunt. Every receipt knows exactly which project it belongs to.
